From Technical Debt to Cognitive and Intent Debt: Rethinking Software Health in the Age of AI
The AI skills gap is here, says AI company, and power users are pulling ahead
articles.description: Anthropic finds AI isn’t replacing jobs yet, but early data shows growing inequality as experienced users gain an edge, raising concerns about future displacement and workforce divides.

articles.title: Convicted spyware chief hints that Greece’s government was behind dozens of phone hacks
articles.description: The spyware founder's comments are the most direct suggestion yet from anyone inside Intellexa that the Mitsotakis government authorized the hacking of dozens of phones belonging to senior Greek government ministers, opposition leaders, military officials, and journalists.

articles.title: Who’s driving Waymo’s self-driving cars? Sometimes, the police.
articles.description: First responders have had to take control of Waymo vehicles and move them during emergency situations, including in at least two active crime scenes, TechCrunch found.

articles.title: Google unveils TurboQuant, a new AI memory compression algorithm — and yes, the internet is calling it ‘Pied Piper’
articles.description: Google’s TurboQuant has the internet joking about Pied Piper from HBO's "Silicon Valley." The compression algorithm promises to shrink AI’s “working memory” by up to 6x, but it’s still just a lab experiment for now.

articles.title: Melania Trump wants a robot to homeschool your child
articles.description: The first lady sees AI and robotics playing a prominent role in the future of American education.

articles.title: Riding the GLP-1 boom, VITL lands $7.5M to overhaul cash-pay clinic prescribing
articles.description: The startup provides an e-prescribing marketplace for the booming cash-pay clinic market.

articles.title: Jury finds Meta and Google negligent in landmark social media addiction trial
articles.description: Evidence presented at the trial swayed the jury to the plaintiff's side, demonstrating that Meta understood how addictive its platforms could be among teens in particular and that it was actively researching the issue and using its findings to increase engagement among young users.

articles.title: Meta is cutting several hundred jobs
articles.description: Meta is laying off several hundred employees across multiple teams, including sales, recruiting, and the Reality Labs division. The cuts will impact employees in the U.S. and other international markets.

articles.title: Apple overhauls its app developer platform with 100 new metrics, more tools
articles.description: Apple is adding 100+ new App Store Connect metrics, giving developers deeper, first-party insights into monetization, subscriptions, and user behavior as it doubles down on its app ecosystem in the AI era.

articles.title: Bernie Sanders and AOC propose a ban on data center construction
articles.description: Senator Bernie Sanders and Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez introduced companion legislation to halt construction on new data centers until Congress passes comprehensive AI regulation.

articles.title: DeleteMe acquires social media security tool Block Party
articles.description: Tracey Chou founded Block Party in 2018 as a tool to help people stay safe from targeted harassment on Twitter, inspired by her own experiences.

articles.title: Google launches Lyria 3 Pro music generation model
articles.description: Google is launching Lyria 3 Pro, an upgraded music model that generates longer, more customizable tracks, as it expands AI music tools across Gemini, enterprise products, and other services.

articles.title: Russia arrests alleged owner of cybercrime forum LeakBase, report says
articles.description: Russian state-owned media reported that police in Russia arrested the administrator of LeakBase, a large hacking forum.

articles.title: Reddit takes on the bots with new ‘human verification’ requirements for fishy behavior
articles.description: Reddit will require suspected automated accounts to verify they’re human, as it ramps up efforts to curb bot-driven spam and manipulation.

articles.title: How soap opera-TikTok hybrids became a billion-dollar business
articles.description: Over the past few years, a new category of mobile apps has quietly exploded into a multi-billion dollar business. They’re called “micro dramas” — short-form, mobile-first scripted shows designed to be watched vertically on your phone. Think soap opera meets TikTok, complete with

digest:
## 🔬 Major Technical Developments

### ARC-AGI-3
URL: https://arcprize.org/arc-agi/3
The benchmark that keeps frontier models honest just got harder.
ARC Prize's third iteration drops as the AI industry grapples with benchmark saturation. This matters because ARC tests genuine reasoning versus pattern matching—current LLMs still struggle here. Watch for which labs engage versus dismiss.

### Google unveils TurboQuant, a new AI memory compression algorithm — and yes, the internet is calling it 'Pied Piper'
URL: https://techcrunch.com/2026/03/25/google-turboquant-ai-memory-compression-silicon-valley-pied-piper/
6x memory compression could reshape inference economics—if it ever leaves the lab.
Google's TurboQuant addresses the real bottleneck in AI deployment: memory, not compute. A 6x reduction in working memory directly translates to smaller GPUs serving larger models. Still experimental, but this is the research direction that determines who can run frontier models profitably at scale.

### Quantization from the Ground Up
URL: https://ngrok.com/blog/quantization
Technical deep-dive arrives as quantization becomes the great equalizer.
Timely primer as the industry splits between those who can afford full-precision inference and everyone else. Understanding quantization tradeoffs is now table stakes for anyone evaluating AI infrastructure plays.


## 💼 Business & Market Signals

### Jury finds Meta and Google negligent in landmark social media addiction trial
URL: https://techcrunch.com/2026/03/25/jury-finds-meta-and-youtube-negligent-in-landmark-social-media-addiction-trial/
Plaintiffs proved Meta knew and weaponized teen addiction data—jury agreed.
This creates litigation template for hundreds of pending cases. The killer evidence: internal research showing Meta understood addiction mechanics and optimized for them anyway. Expect regulatory acceleration and potential product liability exposure across the sector.

### Bernie Sanders and AOC propose a ban on data center construction
URL: https://techcrunch.com/2026/03/25/bernie-sanders-and-aoc-propose-a-ban-on-data-center-construction/
Progressive flank opens second front in AI infrastructure wars.
Won't pass, but signals growing coalition linking AI governance to energy and environmental concerns. This is the political risk that hyperscalers and their REIT partners need to price in—permitting friction is the new constraint, not capital.

### Meta is cutting several hundred jobs
URL: https://techcrunch.com/2026/03/25/meta-is-cutting-several-hundred-jobs/
Reality Labs continues bleeding headcount as metaverse thesis fades.
Cuts span sales, recruiting, and Reality Labs across geographies. Pattern recognition: Meta's efficiency year never ended. The question is whether this represents operating discipline or strategic retreat from hardware ambitions.


## 📋 Case Studies & Deep Dives

### Running Tesla Model 3's computer on my desk using parts from crashed cars
URL: https://bugs.xdavidhu.me/tesla/2026/03/23/running-tesla-model-3s-computer-on-my-desk-using-parts-from-crashed-cars/
Security researcher extracts and runs Tesla's infotainment brain on a bench.
This is how you get access to proprietary automotive systems without NDA constraints. Implications for vulnerability research, competitive intelligence, and the growing secondary market in crashed EV components. Tesla's walled garden has salvage yard side doors.

### 90% of Claude-linked output going to GitHub repos w <2 stars
URL: https://www.claudescode.dev/?window=since_launch
Hard data on where AI-assisted code actually lands—mostly nowhere.
Anthropic's coding tools are generating volume, but the distribution is striking: 90% feeds repos with minimal traction. This is either a long-tail productivity story or evidence that AI coding inflates quantity over quality. Either way, it's the first real usage data on the code-gen gold rush.

### From Technical Debt to Cognitive and Intent Debt: Rethinking Software Health in the Age of AI
URL: https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.22106
Academic framework for what happens when AI writes code humans can't reason about.
Introduces "intent debt"—the gap between what AI-generated code does and what humans understand it to do. As AI code penetration increases, this becomes a real risk category for due diligence on software-heavy acquisitions.


## ⚡ Signal vs. Noise

The week's throughline is infrastructure constraint meeting political friction. Google's TurboQuant and the quantization discourse point to an industry racing to make AI cheaper to run, while Sanders-AOC and the Meta verdict show external forces trying to slow things down. The real opportunity space sits at that intersection: companies that can deliver AI capabilities within tightening energy, regulatory, and liability envelopes. Watch for infrastructure plays that solve for permitting and efficiency simultaneously—that's where the durable moats form.
